issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: blockquote foreground #aaaaaa on background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against the default white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is 2.66:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (minimum 4.5:1). Users with low vision will have difficulty reading quoted content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 330: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users navigating through the page will not see which element has focus unless specific overrides exist for every interactive element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 361: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .feature-card p foreground #aaaaaa on background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card descriptions use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against white backgrounds, yielding 2.66:1 contrast. This is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text, reducing readability for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 948: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset without an outline width or alternative visual indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot perceive focus on buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast)). Users with vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity may experience discomfort from the translateY(1px) transform on active state without a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 534: (add inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended minimum for touch targets. Users with motor impairments or on touch devices may have difficulty activating these buttons without precise aiming.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size: .btn-sm min-height 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm variant sets min-height: 36px, which falls below the 44px recommended minimum for touch targets. Small buttons are difficult to activate for users with tremors or on small screens.

issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word spacing reduces the space between words, impairing readability for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6
issuesFound.explanation: Headings set letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which resolves to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing reduces space between characters, impairing readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 329: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); letter-spacing: normal; color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-wrap: balance; }

issuesFound.explanation: Feature card body text uses #aaaaaa on a #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.85:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a foreground #94a3b8 / background #555555
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use #94a3b8 on a #555555 background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.7:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1599: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Footer and social links (Lines 1599, 1621) do not regain underlines on hover, relying solely on colour changes which may not be perceivable by all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds the error element to aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, causing users to miss validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2151 (after addDescribedBy): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer and removes the hidden attribute from the overlay, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive that the menu has expanded because the ARIA state remains stale.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2932 (after _open = true;): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates the WAI-ARIA disclosure pattern and leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3103 (after inst.open = true;): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function updates CSS classes to indicate the active filter tab but fails to update the aria-pressed (or aria-selected) attribute on the buttons. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active because the state is not communicated programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3804 (after toggleClass): Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function animates the FAQ panel height and sets CSS classes, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state of the accordion item to blind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4555 (after item.open = true;): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing role enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter attaches click handlers to [data-filter-value] elements and manages roving tabindex, but does not verify these elements are buttons or have role="button". If implemented as divs or spans in the HTML, they will be inaccessible to keyboard users despite the tabindex management.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility and adds classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance function sets aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to incorrectly report the dropdown state as closed when it is actually open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens via _openInstance, the change in visible content is not announced to screen readers. While mobileNav and faqAccordion use liveRegion.announce for similar actions, dropdownMenu does not, leaving users unaware that new options are available.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function sets aria-expanded="false", resulting in inaccurate state reporting for assistive technology users when an FAQ item is opened.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates does not update aria-pressed on filter buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function updates the visual active state of filter buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Since these are buttons acting as a single-select group without role="tab", aria-pressed is required to convey the selected state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. The corresponding markValid function removes aria-invalid, implying the invalid state should be set here. Without it, screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function calls srUtils.markInvalid() which only links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the input. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2410: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the else block (when result.valid is false), and Line 2405: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` inside the if block (when valid).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer and overlay but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the menu, leaving users unaware that the drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals a dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern and prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1255: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function animates the panel open and updates CSS classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the answer panel is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2805: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed/aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle active states to show the selected category but only update CSS classes. They lack aria-pressed (for buttons) or aria-selected (for tabs), so screen readers cannot convey which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2105: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` or `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after toggleClass.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form uses inlineValidator which fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the email field when validation errors occur. Assistive technologies cannot detect the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2410: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` when rendering invalid state, and `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` when valid.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form validation via inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields, preventing screen readers from announcing field errors to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2410: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` for invalid fields and `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` for valid fields.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). The drawer opens without trapping focus, allowing keyboard users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1190: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true });` after checking that _drawer exists.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing dialog role
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog but lacks role="dialog". Screen readers may not identify it as a modal window requiring user interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'role', 'dialog');` and `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-label', 'Mobile navigation');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open function adds the open class and locks scroll but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1435: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open helper function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. The close helper sets it to "false", leaving the expanded state unannounced to assistive technologies when the dropdown opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1635: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function sets the item state and removes hidden from the panel but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", resulting in incorrect state reporting for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2450: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function for marking fields invalid only adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying the field as invalid, relying solely on the description which may not be sufficient.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1200: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner function does not announce banner appearance
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is dynamically inserted and shown, no announcement is made to the live region. Screen reader users may be unaware that a modal dialog requiring interaction has appeared over the content.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3035: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.bannerMessage || 'Cookie consent settings available');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onAccept function does not announce acceptance
issuesFound.explanation: When the user accepts cookies, the banner hides but no confirmation is announced via the live region. Users are not informed that their preference was saved successfully.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3015: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onDecline function does not announce declination
issuesFound.explanation: When the user declines non-essential cookies, the action completes without a live region announcement. Screen reader users receive no feedback that their selection was processed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3025: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved');

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator._animate function does not announce count updates
issuesFound.explanation: The statistic counter animates the number visually and updates aria-label on completion, but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the count finishes animating to its final value.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2895: liveRegion.announce(`${cfg.prefix}${cfg.target}${cfg.suffix}`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing toggle missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.init function configures the billing period toggle element (data-billing-toggle) with role="switch" and keyboard handlers, but it does not provide an aria-label. Since this control may be rendered as an icon-only button or div, it will be inaccessible to screen readers without a label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3428: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.switchToMonthly || 'Switch billing period');` after the line `if (!_toggleEl.getAttribute('role')) _toggleEl.setAttribute('role', 'switch');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode buttons missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.init function sets up view mode toggle buttons (data-view-mode) with aria-pressed states, but it does not ensure these buttons have accessible names. If the buttons rely solely on iconography without text, they will be inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2985: Add code to set aria-label on view mode buttons: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(btn => { if (!btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) { const mode = btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode'); setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', mode === 'list' ? 'Switch to list view' : 'Switch to grid view'); } });`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period tab buttons missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.init function discovers period selection buttons (data-period) and configures them with aria-selected, but it does not ensure they have accessible names. These buttons need aria-labels to indicate which billing period they select.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3438: Add code to set aria-label: `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const period = btn.getAttribute('data-period'); if (!btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) { setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', `Select ${BILLING_PERIODS[period]?.label || period} billing`); } });`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function ensures the trigger button has aria-expanded and aria-controls, but it does not verify or set an aria-label. If the button is an icon-only hamburger menu, it will be inaccessible without a label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1730: Add `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label')) { setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.openMenu || 'Open navigation menu'); }` after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and locks scroll but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class to the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to miss the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function changes visible content (opens the panel) but does not write to an aria-live region. Users relying on screen readers are not notified when a dropdown menu opens, unlike the mobileNav and faqAccordion modules which do announce.

The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", resulting in incorrect state reporting to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to indicate error states but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine when the input contains invalid data, preventing users from identifying form errors.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar visual clone missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.fix:
Line IntegrationsBar clone ul:

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button element that toggles mobile navigation sections uses the isExpanded state but does not communicate this state to assistive technology via aria-expanded.

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component renders individual star characters (★☆) inside spans. Since the parent span already provides an aria-label with the rating value, the individual stars are decorative and should be hidden from assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons track an active/selected state (activeFilter === tab.value) but do not communicate this state to assistive technology using aria-pressed.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons track selection state (value === 'monthly' / value === 'annual') but lack aria-pressed to indicate which option is active.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The video play/pause button tracks isPlaying state but lacks aria-pressed to communicate the pressed/released state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~930: Add aria-pressed to the Button.
  ```
  <Button
    variant="ghost"
    size="sm"
    aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'}
    aria-pressed={isPlaying}
    onClick={handleTogglePlay}
    className="hero-section__video-toggle"
  >
  ```

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Active navigation links in DesktopNav use a visual className indicator but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify the current page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 625: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active links missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Active navigation links in MobileNav use a visual className indicator but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify the current page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The cloned ul element in IntegrationsBar used for visual looping lacks aria-hidden="true". This causes duplicate content to be announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1090: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: StarRating inner star spans missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star spans inside StarRating lack aria-hidden="true". The container has an aria-label, so the individual characters are decorative and should be hidden to prevent redundant announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 470: <span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">
